carbon.$description: Carbon — Primary text, body copy, nav links, icons, logo wordmark, footer surface, and the near-black anchoring tone for the entire monochrome system

paper.$description: Paper — Page canvas, card backgrounds, product tile surfaces, input fields, and the dominant background that makes photography the only chromatic voice

ash-gray.$description: Ash Gray — Subtle icon strokes, secondary borders, search underline at rest, placeholder ghost text in inputs — the quietest non-text neutral

stone.$description: Stone — Hover wash on nav strips, secondary surface tone for utility bars and announcement backgrounds, the softest surface lift in the stack

graphite.$description: Graphite — Muted helper text, inactive form borders, disabled icon strokes — the mid-gray that signals de-emphasis without losing legibility

smoke.$description: Smoke — Hairline dividers between product rows, thin section separators, the barely-visible structural line in an otherwise seamless layout

farfetch-basis.$description: Custom geometric sans-serif used for everything user-facing: nav links at 13–15px/400, body and product labels at 15px/400, section titles at 22–30px/400, and the FARFETCH wordmark at 700. The 400-weight-only headings are a deliberate anti-bold move — section titles rest at regular weight, confident without shouting, while 700 is reserved for the wordmark and the most emphatic category labels. The custom geometry brings even stroke contrast and subtle humanist warmth that a system Helvetica wouldn't.

canvas.$description: Surface level 0: Page background — the infinite white wall of the gallery

wash.$description: Surface level 1: Utility bar and hover state for nav strips — the only non-white surface that ever appears

divider.$description: Surface level 2: Hairline structural separator between product grid rows and footer sections

inversion.$description: Surface level 3: Footer background and any high-contrast terminal surface — used sparingly to close the page
